title: Manual Cell Counting
author: Aparna Kumar
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5zng75e

steps.title: Remove 10 µL of the solution...
steps.instruction: Remove 10 µL of the solution containing the single cells using a p20 micropipette and add to a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube or onto a piece of parafilm.

steps.title: Centrifuge the Trypan Blue and remove...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the Trypan Blue and remove 10 µL from the top of the Trypan Blue as not to get any debris using a p20 micropipette.

steps.title: Add the 10 µL of Trypan Blue to the 1
steps.instruction: Add the 10 µL of Trypan Blue to the 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube or the parafilm conataining the cells and gently mix by pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Wipe the hemocytometer and a matching...
steps.instruction: Wipe the hemocytometer and a matching coverslip with 70% ethanol and place in the biosafety cabinet.

steps.title: Place the coverslip onto the center of the hemocytometer
steps.instruction: Place the coverslip onto the center of the hemocytometer. Figure Legend: Hemocytometer with coverslip.

steps.title: Remove 10 µL of solution from the 1
steps.instruction: Remove 10 µL of solution from the 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube or the parafilm using a p20 micropipette.

steps.title: Place the tip of the pipette...
steps.instruction: Place the tip of the pipette at the bottom edge of the coverslip and slowly pipette the solution into the hemocytometer. The bottom chamber should be filled.

steps.title: Remove another 10 µL of solution...
steps.instruction: Remove another 10 µL of solution and add to the top chamber by placing the tip of the pipette at the top edge of the coverslip and slowly pipetting the solution into the top chamber.

steps.title: Place the filled hemocytometer on a...
steps.instruction: Place the filled hemocytometer on a microscope and focus the microscope such that the cells and the grid lines in each chamber are clearly visible.

steps.title: Pick one of the 9 boxes...
steps.instruction: Pick one of the 9 boxes in the top grid (the grid in the top chamber) to begin counting. Note: Cells stained blue are dead cells and should only be counted to determine the viability, not the cell count. Figure Legend: Live vs Dead cells.

steps.title: Any cells that are on the...
steps.instruction: Any cells that are on the bottom and right edge lines should be counted while any cells overlapping the top and left edge should not be counted. Figure Legend: Cell counting grid.

steps.title: Using a tally counter, keep track...
steps.instruction: Using a tally counter, keep track of the number of cells counted in one box.

steps.title: Note the total cells in one...
steps.instruction: Note the total cells in one box and reset the counter.

steps.title: Repeat steps 10 - 13 four...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 10 - 13 four more times, choosing a different box in the same grid.

steps.title: Move on to the bottome grid...
steps.instruction: Move on to the bottome grid (the grid in the bottom chamber) and repeat the process of counting by counting the cells in 5 boxes and making a note of the counts.

steps.title: Find the average number of cells...
steps.instruction: Find the average number of cells per box by adding up the total number of cells and dividing by the number of boxes that where counted. Note: In this protocol, 10 boxes where counted total, 5 in the top grid and 5 in the bottom grid, so the total number of cells should be divided by 10.

steps.title: Multiply the final number from step...
steps.instruction: Multiply the final number from step 16 by 2 and then by 10,000. The result is the total number cells in the original solution (before the addition of Trypan Blue) per mL.

steps.title: Total number of cells = (number...
steps.instruction: Total number of cells = (number of cells per mL) * (mL of original solution containing the cells)
